help.title: How to configure Google BigQuery datasource?
help.markdown:
##### Uploading Google Service Account key

Create a [Google Cloud Platform (GCP) Service Account](https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/creating-managing-service-accounts) on the project you want to show data. The **BigQuery Data Viewer** role and the **Job User** role provide all the permissions that Grafana needs. The [BigQuery API](https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/library/bigquery.googleapis.com) has to be enabled on GCP for the data source to work.

##### Using GCE Default Service Account

When Grafana is running on a Google Compute Engine (GCE) virtual machine, it is possible for Grafana to automatically retrieve the default project id and authentication token from the metadata server. For this to work, you need to make sure that you have a service account that is setup as the default account for the virtual machine and that the service account has been given read access to the BigQuery API.

**Note that, Grafana data source integrates with a single GCP project. If you need to visualize data from multiple GCP projects, create one data source per GCP project.**

fields.label: JWT token
fields.description: Upload or paste a Google service-account JWT key file (.json). Its project_id, client_email, token_uri, and private_key are distributed into the JWT fields below.

fields.label: Private key path
fields.description: Paste private key or provide path to private file

ui.placeholder: File location of your private key (e.g. /etc/secrets/gce.pem)

fields.label: Private key
fields.description: Paste private key or provide path to private file

fields.label: Enable
fields.description: Enable service account impersonation. Read more about service account impersonation here: https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/service-account-impersonation

fields.label: Workload Identity Pool Provider
fields.description: Full resource name of the workload identity pool provider (e.g. projects/123/locations/global/workloadIdentityPools/my-pool/providers/my-provider)

fields.label: Service account email
fields.description: Optional. If set, the federated identity impersonates this service account when calling Google APIs.

Automatically set to true when authenticationType is 'forwardOAuthIdentity' or 'workloadIdentityFederation'; otherwise false. Written by AuthConfig.tsx:73-74 as a side-effect of the auth-type radio, not by a direct UI toggle.

fields.label: Processing location
fields.description: Read more about processing location here: https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/locations

fields.label: Service endpoint
fields.description: Specifies the network address of an API service. Read more about service endpoint here: https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/reference/rest#service-endpoint

fields.label: Max bytes billed
fields.description: Prevent queries that would process more than this amount of bytes. Read more about max bytes billed here: https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/best-practices-costs

Defined in the backend Settings struct (pkg/bigquery/types/types.go:13) but not read by any current code path and not exposed in the configuration editor. Kept in the schema so provisioning payloads are validated instead of silently accepting unknown keys.

Defined in the backend Settings struct (pkg/bigquery/types/types.go:15) as the desired default query priority (INTERACTIVE / BATCH). Not read by any current code path and not exposed in the datasource configuration editor (a queryPriority also exists on individual queries at src/types.ts:107 but that is a separate storage location).

relationships.description: Google JWT File credentials — required as a set for jwt auth

Alternative sources for the JWT private key: either supply the key inline as a secret (privateKey) or point at a file path on the Grafana server (privateKeyPath). Exactly one of the two must be configured for jwt auth.
Service account impersonation: setting serviceAccountToImpersonate is only effective when usingImpersonation is true (pkg/bigquery/http_client.go:53-58,73-88)
Workload Identity Federation credentials — workloadIdentityPoolProvider is required for WIF auth; wifServiceAccountEmail is optional impersonation

instructions.msg: Four authentication methods are available, selected via jsonData.authenticationType: 'jwt' (Google JWT File, the default), 'gce' (GCE Default Service Account, only works when Grafana runs on a Google Compute Engine VM), 'forwardOAuthIdentity' (Forward the caller's OAuth token to BigQuery), and 'workloadIdentityFederation' (only visible in Grafana Cloud, but the backend accepts it anywhere). Configure exactly one method; the backend switches on this discriminator (pkg/bigquery/http_client.go:51-89) and only the credentials of the selected method are used.

To configure Google JWT File authentication, set jsonData.authenticationType to 'jwt' and provide jsonData.defaultProject, jsonData.clientEmail, jsonData.tokenUri, and the service account's private key. The private key may be supplied inline as secureJsonData.privateKey OR by pointing jsonData.privateKeyPath at a file on the Grafana server. Minimal inline payload: {"jsonData":{"authenticationType":"jwt","defaultProject":"<project>","clientEmail":"<sa email>","tokenUri":"https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token"},"secureJsonData":{"privateKey":"-----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY-----
...
-----END PRIVATE KEY-----
"}}. Grant the service account the 'BigQuery Data Viewer' and 'Job User' roles on the project, and enable the BigQuery API.

To configure GCE Default Service Account authentication, set jsonData.authenticationType to 'gce'. Minimal payload: {"jsonData":{"authenticationType":"gce","defaultProject":"<project>"}}. The backend obtains credentials from the GCE metadata server; this only works when Grafana runs on a Google Compute Engine VM whose default service account has been granted the required BigQuery permissions.

To configure Workload Identity Federation authentication, set jsonData.authenticationType to 'workloadIdentityFederation' and provide jsonData.workloadIdentityPoolProvider (mandatory; missing it makes the backend fail at pkg/bigquery/http_client.go:95). Optionally set jsonData.wifServiceAccountEmail to have the federated identity impersonate a specific service account. To configure Forward OAuth Identity, set jsonData.authenticationType to 'forwardOAuthIdentity' — no additional fields are required; the backend forwards the caller's OAuth token (jsonData.oauthPassThru is set to true as a side-effect and enables http_client.ForwardHTTPHeaders).

secureJsonData values (privateKey) are write-only: they can be set or reset but never read back. To check whether the private key is already configured on an existing datasource, read secureJsonFields.privateKey (true/false) instead of secureJsonData.privateKey.

Optional query behaviour: jsonData.processingLocation restricts BigQuery job processing to a specific region (empty string = automatic selection; see https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/locations for the list). jsonData.serviceEndpoint overrides the default https://bigquery.googleapis.com/bigquery/v2/ URL (for private-service-connect / regional endpoints). jsonData.MaxBytesBilled caps the bytes any query may bill (integer bytes, e.g. 5242880 = 5 MB). Two fields are declared in the backend Settings struct but not read at runtime: jsonData.flatRateProject and jsonData.queryPriority — do not populate them for new datasources.
